items.title: Porsche is adding an all-electric Cayenne coupe to its lineup
items.summary: Porsche will start selling the Cayenne Coupe Electric in late summer.

items.title: Bob Iger rejoins Thrive Capital as advisor after Disney exit
items.summary: The former Disney CEO hold a stake in Thrive, and has previously held the role of the venture partner at the firm.

items.title: Authorities arrest special forces soldier who allegedly made $400K on Polymarket bet involving Maduro operation
items.summary: The soldier is accused of using classified government information to inform a wager on the prediction market Polymarket.

items.title: Redwood Materials loses COO amid layoffs, restructuring
items.summary: Former Tesla executive Chris Lister is retiring, and at least three other VPs have left Redwood around the recent restructuring, TechCrunch has learned.

items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
items.summary: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
items.summary: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

items.title: Instagram tests a new ‘Instants’ app for sharing disappearing photos
items.summary: The app lets users share disappearing photos with their friends that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

items.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
items.summary: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

items.title: Trump’s pick to run US cyber agency CISA asks to drop out
items.summary: Sean Plankey has requested to withdraw his name to run the U.S. cybersecurity agency after a tumultuous year of chaotic temporary leadership.

items.title: Don’t stop hiring humans — stop hiring the wrong humans, Artisan’s founder says
items.summary: Artisan may be known for their bold “Stop Hiring Humans” campaign but the reality is every founder needs to assemble the right team if they want to scale.

items.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
items.summary: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

items.title: Meta to cut 10% of jobs, or 8,000 employees, report says
items.summary: According to an internal memo, Meta plans to begin its sweeping layoffs on May 20.

items.title: Bluesky now supports better-quality photos
items.summary: The max size is now 2MB and max resolution is 4000 x 4000.

items.title: Microsoft offers buyout for up to 7% of US employees
items.summary: If a worker's years of service at Microsoft plus their age equals 70 or more, they will be eligible for a voluntary retirement buyout.

items.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
items.summary: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

items.title: Meta is revamping its cross-app management system
items.summary: Meta's Accounts Center will transition to the Meta Account, which is supposed to make it easier to manage your experience on various Meta apps and devices.

items.title: X is shutting down Communities because of low usage and lots of spam
items.summary: X Communities are no more. Only a fraction of X users were using Communities, and much of that use was spam, the company said.

items.title: Salmon raises $100M in equity and debt to bring digital credit to underbanked Filipinos
items.summary: Salmon is targeting underbanked Filipinos, with little to no credit history, as well as people unhappy with the incumbent lenders' reliability issues.

items.title: Vercel says some of its customers’ data was stolen prior to its recent hack
items.summary: The app and website hosting company has found evidence of a second compromise of customer accounts after expanding its initial investigation following a breach in early April.

items.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
items.summary: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.
